rel.description:
Specifies the relationship of the linked URL as space-separated link types. Possible values:

* `alternate`: An alternate version of the current document.
* `author`: The author of the current document or article.
* `bookmark`: The permalink for the nearest ancestor section.
* `external`: The referenced document is not part of the same site as the current document.
* `help`: A link to context-sensitive help.
* `license`: Content covered by the copyright license described by the referenced document.
* `me`: Links to content owned by the current document's author.
* `next`: The next document in the series.
* `nofollow`: Not endorsed by the original author or publisher.
* `noreferrer`: Prevents the `Referer` header from being sent. Same effect as `noopener`.
* `opener`: Creates an auxiliary browsing context if the hyperlink would otherwise create a top-level browsing context that is not an auxiliary one (i.e., when `target="_blank"`).
* `prev`: The previous document in the series.
* `search`: Links to a resource that can be used to search through the current document and its related pages.
* `tag`: Marks the current document with the given tag.

**Note**: Only available when `href` is specified.

fit.description:
Image resizing method, similar to the CSS [`object-fit`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/object-fit) property. Possible values:

* `contain`: Scales the image to fit within the box while preserving aspect ratio. The image will be "letterboxed" if the aspect ratios do not match.
* `cover`: Scales the image to fill the box while preserving aspect ratio. The image will be clipped if the aspect ratios do not match.
* `fill`: Default. Scales the image to fill the box; the image may be stretched if aspect ratios do not match.
* `none`: No resizing.
* `scale-down`: Scales the image down to fit. Behaves like `none` or `contain`, whichever results in the smaller image.

variant.description:

enum.secondary: Uses the secondary container background color.

size.description:

extended.description: Extends the FAB to show text alongside the icon.

events.focus: Emitted when the FAB gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the FAB loses focus.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

placement.description:
Determines where the component is placed. Possible values:

* `top`: Places the component at the top.
* `bottom`: Places the component at the bottom.
* `left`: Places the component on the left.
* `right`: Places the component on the right.

enum.top: Places the component at the top.
enum.bottom: Places the component at the bottom.
enum.left: Places the component on the left.
enum.right: Places the component on the right.

max.description: Sets the maximum value for the progress indicator. The default value is `1`.

value.description: Sets the current value of the progress indicator. If not specified, the progress indicator is in an indeterminate state.


mdui-list-item.summary:
List Item Component. Use with the `<mdui-list>` component.

```html
<mdui-list>

..<mdui-list-item>Item 1</mdui-list-item>
..<mdui-list-item>Item 2</mdui-list-item>
</mdui-list>
```

headline.description: Main text. Alternatively, use the default slot.

headlineLine.description:
Maximum number of lines for the main text. Overflow text is truncated. Default is no limit. Possible values:

* `1`: Single-line text that truncates when it overflows.
* `2`: Two-line text that truncates when it overflows.
* `3`: Three-line text that truncates when it overflows.

enum.1: Single-line text that truncates when it overflows.
enum.2: Two-line text that truncates when it overflows.
enum.3: Three-line text that truncates when it overflows.

descriptionLine.description:
Maximum number of lines for the subtext. Overflow text is truncated. Default is no limit. Possible values:

* `1`: Single-line text that truncates when it overflows.
* `2`: Two-line text that truncates when it overflows.
* `3`: Three-line text that truncates when it overflows.

enum.1: Single-line text that truncates when it overflows.
enum.2: Two-line text that truncates when it overflows.
enum.3: Three-line text that truncates when it overflows.

icon.description: Material Icons name on the left. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`.

endIcon.description: Material Icons name on the right. Alternatively, use `slot="end-icon"`.

disabled.description: Disables the list item. It dims the item and disables interactive elements like [`<mdui-checkbox>`](/docs/2/components/checkbox), [`<mdui-radio>`](/docs/2/components/radio), and [`<mdui-switch>`](/docs/2/components/switch).

active.description: Marks the list item as active.

nonclickable.description: Disables the list item's default click action, but interactive elements like [`<mdui-checkbox>`](/docs/2/components/checkbox), [`<mdui-radio>`](/docs/2/components/radio), and [`<mdui-switch>`](/docs/2/components/switch) inside it remain functional.

rounded.description: Applies a rounded appearance to the list item.

alignment.description:
Vertical alignment of the list item. Possible values:

* `start`: Aligns to the top.
* `center`: Aligns to the center.
* `end`: Aligns to the bottom.

events.focus: Emitted when the list item gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the list item loses focus.

slots.icon: Element on the left of the list item.
slots.end-icon: Element on the right of the list item.

mdui-menu.summary:
Menu Component. Use with the `<mdui-menu-item>` component.

```html
<mdui-menu>
..<mdui-menu-item>Item 1</mdui-menu-item>
..<mdui-menu-item>Item 2</mdui-menu-item>
</mdui-menu>
```

selects.description:
Controls whether menu items can be selected. They are not selectable by default. Possible values:

* `single`: Only one item can be selected at a time.
* `multiple`: Multiple items can be selected.

enum.single: Only one item can be selected at a time.

value.description:
The value of the selected `<mdui-menu-item>`.

**Note**: The HTML attribute always accepts a string and can only be used as an initial value when `selects="single"`. The JavaScript property is a string when `selects="single"` and an array of strings when `selects="multiple"`. When `selects="multiple"`, update the JavaScript property to change this value.

dense.description: Indicates whether the menu items use a compact layout.

submenuTrigger.description:
Defines how submenus open. Multiple space-separated values are supported. Possible values:

* `click`: Opens the submenu when the menu item is clicked.
* `hover`: Opens the submenu when hovering over a menu item.
* `focus`: Opens the submenu when the menu item receives focus.
* `manual`: Submenus can only be opened and closed programmatically; no other trigger methods can be specified.

enum.click: Opens the submenu when the menu item is clicked.
enum.hover: Opens the submenu when hovering over a menu item.
enum.focus: Opens the submenu when the menu item receives focus.
enum.manual: Submenus can only be opened and closed programmatically; no other trigger methods can be specified.

submenuOpenDelay.description: The delay (in milliseconds) before a submenu opens on hover.

submenuCloseDelay.description: The delay (in milliseconds) before a submenu closes on hover.

events.change: Emitted when the selected state of menu items changes.

slots: Submenu items (`<mdui-menu-item>`), dividers ([`<mdui-divider>`](/docs/2/components/divider)), and other elements.

cssProperties.--shape-corner: The corner radius of the component. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).

value.description: The value of the menu item.

icon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the left icon. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`. An empty string reserves space for an icon.

endIcon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the right icon. Alternatively, use `slot="end-icon"`.

endText.description: Specifies the right text. Alternatively, use `slot="end-text"`.

selectedIcon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the selected state. Alternatively, use `slot="selected-icon"`.

events.focus: Emitted when the menu item gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the menu item loses focus.
events.submenu-open: Emitted when the submenu starts to open. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.submenu-opened: Emitted after the submenu has opened and the animations are completed.
events.submenu-close: Emitted when the submenu starts to close. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.submenu-closed: Emitted after the submenu has closed and the animations are completed.

Navigation Bar Component. Use with the `<mdui-navigation-bar-item>` component.

```html
</mdui-navigation-bar>
```

hide.description: Whether the navigation bar is hidden.

labelVisibility.description:
Specifies when the text is shown. Possible values:

* `auto`: Visible if there are 3 or fewer items; otherwise, only visible in the selected state.
* `selected`: Only visible in the selected state.
* `labeled`: Always visible.
* `unlabeled`: Never visible.

enum.auto: Visible if there are 3 or fewer items; otherwise, only visible in the selected state.
enum.selected: Only visible in the selected state.

value.description: The value of the selected `<mdui-navigation-bar-item>`.

scrollBehavior.description:
Defines the scroll behavior. Possible values:

* `hide`: Hides when scrolling.

events.show: Emitted when the navigation bar starts to show. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.shown: Emitted after the navigation bar has shown and animations are complete.
events.hide: Emitted when the navigation bar starts to hide. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.hidden: Emitted after the navigation bar has hidden and animations are complete.

cssProperties.--shape-corner: The corner radius of the component. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--z-index: The CSS `z-index` value of the component.

icon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the inactive state. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`.

activeIcon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the active state. Alternatively, use `slot="active-icon"`.

value.description: The value of the navigation item.

events.focus: Emitted when the navigation bar item gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the navigation bar item loses focus.

cssProperties.--shape-corner-indicator: The corner radius of the indicator. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).

modal.description:
Displays an overlay when open.

On narrow devices (screen width < [`--mdui-breakpoint-md`](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#breakpoint)), the overlay is always displayed.

closeOnEsc.description: Closes the drawer when the ESC key is pressed and an overlay is present.

closeOnOverlayClick.description: Closes the drawer when the overlay is clicked.

placement.description:
Sets the drawer's position. Possible values:

contained.description:
By default, the navigation drawer is positioned relative to the `body` element. If set, it is positioned relative to its parent element.

**Note**: You must manually set `position: relative; overflow: hidden;` on the parent element when this attribute is set.

events.open: Emitted when the navigation drawer starts to open. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.opened: Emitted after the navigation drawer has opened and the animations are completed.
events.close: Emitted when the navigation drawer starts to close. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.closed: Emitted after the navigation drawer has closed and the animations are completed.
events.overlay-click: Emitted when the overlay is clicked.

cssProperties.--z-index: The CSS `z-index` value of the component.

value.description: Specifies the value of the radio.

checked.description: Sets the radio to the checked state.

uncheckedIcon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the unchecked state. Alternatively, use `slot="unchecked-icon"`.

checkedIcon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name for the checked state. Alternatively, use `slot="checked-icon"`.

mdui-radio-group.summary:
Radio Group Component. Use with the `<mdui-radio>` component.

```html
<mdui-radio-group value="chinese">
..<mdui-radio value="chinese">Chinese</mdui-radio>
..<mdui-radio value="english">English</mdui-radio>
</mdui-radio-group>
```

form.description:
Associates the radio group with a `<form>` element. Set this to the `id` of a `<form>` in the same document. If omitted, the radio group uses its parent `<form>`, if any.

This lets the radio group work with any form in the document, not just the one it is nested in.

name.description: The name of the radio group, which is submitted with form data.

value.description: The value of the selected radio button, which is submitted with form data.

defaultValue.description: The default selected value. The radio group resets to this value when the form is reset. JavaScript only.

required.description: Requires a radio selection when the form is submitted.

validity.description: A [`ValidityState`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ValidityState) object that represents the element's validity states.

validationMessage.description: Validation message. Empty string when valid.

methods.checkValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event and returns `false`. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.reportValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event, returns `false`, and displays a validation message. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.setCustomValidity: Sets a custom error message. If the message is non-empty, the field is considered invalid.

events.change: Emitted when the selected value changes.
events.input: Emitted when the selected value changes.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

defaultValue.description: Specifies the default value. The slider resets to this state when the form is reset. JavaScript only.

value.description:

**Note**: This value cannot be set initially via an HTML attribute. Use the JavaScript property to change it.

events.focus: Emitted when the range slider gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the range slider loses focus.
events.change: Emitted when the value changes and the range slider loses focus.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

variant.description:
Defines the select variant. Possible values:

* `filled`: Solid background with strong visual emphasis.
* `outlined`: Bordered with lighter visual emphasis.

enum.filled: Solid background with strong visual emphasis.

name.description: Name of the select, which is submitted with form data.

value.description:

If `multiple` is not set, the value is a string; otherwise, it is an array of strings. HTML attributes can only set string values; array values must be set via the JavaScript property.

defaultValue.description: Default selected value. The select resets to this value when the form is reset. JavaScript only.

* `auto`: Automatically determined.
* `bottom`: Below the input.
* `top`: Above the input.

prefix.description: Prefix text of the select. Displayed only when the select is focused or has a value. Alternatively, use `slot="prefix"`.

suffix.description: Suffix text of the select. Displayed only when the select is focused or has a value. Alternatively, use `slot="suffix"`.

icon.description: Material Icons name for the prefix icon. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`.

endIcon.description: Material Icons name for the suffix icon. Alternatively, use `slot="end-icon"`.

errorIcon.description: Material Icons name displayed on the right when form field validation fails. Alternatively, use `slot="error-icon"`.

form.description:
Associates the select with a `<form>` element. Set this to the `id` of a `<form>` in the same document. If omitted, the select uses its parent `<form>`, if any.

This lets the select work with any form in the document, not just the one it is nested in.

required.description: Requires a selection when the form is submitted.

validity.description: A [`ValidityState`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ValidityState) object that represents the element's validity states.

methods.checkValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event and returns `false`. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.reportValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event, returns `false`, and displays a validation message. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.setCustomValidity: Sets a custom error message. If the message is non-empty, the field is considered invalid.

events.focus: Emitted when the select gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the select loses focus.
events.change: Emitted when the selected value changes.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

cssParts.text-field__prefix: Text on the left side of the text field.
cssParts.text-field__suffix: Text on the right side of the text field.

defaultValue.description: The default value. The slider resets to this value when the form is reset. JavaScript only.

events.focus: Emitted when the slider gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the slider loses focus.
events.change: Emitted when the value changes and the slider loses focus.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

placement.description:
Snackbar placement. Default is `bottom`. Possible values:

* `top`: Top center.
* `top-start`: Top left.
* `top-end`: Top right.
* `bottom`: Bottom center.
* `bottom-start`: Bottom left.
* `bottom-end`: Bottom right.

action.description: Text for the action button. Alternatively, use `slot="action"`.

actionLoading.description: Whether the action button is in a loading state.

closeable.description: Shows a close button on the right.

closeIcon.description: Material Icons name for the close button. Alternatively, use `slot="close-icon"`.

messageLine.description:
Maximum lines for message text. Default is unlimited. Possible values:

* `1`: Single line.
* `2`: Two lines.

autoCloseDelay.description: Automatically closes the Snackbar after the given delay (in milliseconds). Set to `0` to disable auto-close. Default is `5000`.

closeOnOutsideClick.description: Closes the Snackbar when the user clicks or touches outside it.

events.open: Emitted when the snackbar starts to open. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.opened: Emitted after the snackbar opens and animations complete.
events.close: Emitted when the snackbar starts to close. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.closed: Emitted after the snackbar closes and animations complete.
events.action-click: Emitted when the action button is clicked.

slots.close-button: Right-side close button. Shown if `closeable` is set.
slots.close-icon: Icon in the right close button.

cssProperties.--shape-corner: The corner radius of the component. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--z-index: The CSS `z-index` value of the component.

checked.description: Sets the switch to the checked state.

defaultChecked.description: The default checked state. The switch resets to this state when the form is reset. JavaScript only.

uncheckedIcon.description: The Material Icons name for the unchecked state. Alternatively, use `slot="unchecked-icon"`.

checkedIcon.description: The Material Icons name for the checked state. Alternatively, use `slot="checked-icon"`. Defaults to the `check` icon; setting an empty string removes the default icon.

required.description: The switch must be checked before the form is submitted.

form.description:
Associates the switch with a `<form>` element. Set this to the `id` of a `<form>` in the same document. If omitted, the switch uses its parent `<form>`, if any.

This lets the switch work with any form in the document, not just the one it is nested in.

name.description: The name of the switch, which is submitted with form data.

value.description: The value of the switch, which is submitted with form data.

validity.description: A [`ValidityState`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ValidityState) object that represents the element's validity states.

validationMessage.description: Validation message. Empty string when valid.

methods.checkValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event and returns `false`. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.reportValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event, returns `false`, and displays a validation message. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.setCustomValidity: Sets a custom error message. If the message is non-empty, the field is considered invalid.

events.focus: Emitted when the switch gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the switch loses focus.
events.change: Emitted when the checked state changes.
events.input: Emitted when the checked state changes.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.

cssProperties.--shape-corner: The corner radius of the track. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--shape-corner-thumb: The corner radius of the icon container. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).

mdui-tab.summary:
Tab Component. Use with the `<mdui-tabs>` and `<mdui-tab-panel>` components.

```html
<mdui-tabs value="tab-1">
..<mdui-tab value="tab-1">Tab 1</mdui-tab>
..<mdui-tab value="tab-2">Tab 2</mdui-tab>
..<mdui-tab value="tab-3">Tab 3</mdui-tab>

..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-1">Panel 1</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-2">Panel 2</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-3">Panel 3</mdui-tab-panel>
</mdui-tabs>
```

value.description: Specifies the tab value.

icon.description: Specifies the Material Icons name. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`.

inline.description: Arranges the icon and text horizontally.

events.focus: Emitted when the tab gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the tab loses focus.

mdui-tab-panel.summary:
Tab Panel Component. Use with the `<mdui-tabs>` and `<mdui-tab>` components.

```html
<mdui-tabs value="tab-1">

..<mdui-tab value="tab-2">Tab 2</mdui-tab>
..<mdui-tab value="tab-3">Tab 3</mdui-tab>

..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-1">Panel 1</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-2">Panel 2</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-3">Panel 3</mdui-tab-panel>
</mdui-tabs>
```

value.description: Specifies the value of the tab panel.

slots: The content of the tab panel.

mdui-tabs.summary:
Tabs Component. Use with the `<mdui-tab>` and `<mdui-tab-panel>` components.

```html
<mdui-tabs value="tab-1">
..<mdui-tab value="tab-1">Tab 1</mdui-tab>
..<mdui-tab value="tab-2">Tab 2</mdui-tab>
..<mdui-tab value="tab-3">Tab 3</mdui-tab>

..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-1">Panel 1</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-2">Panel 2</mdui-tab-panel>
..<mdui-tab-panel slot="panel" value="tab-3">Panel 3</mdui-tab-panel>
</mdui-tabs>
```

variant.description:
Defines the tab variant. Possible values:

* `primary`: Sits below `<mdui-top-app-bar>` and is used to switch between main application pages.
* `secondary`: Sits within the page and is used to switch between related content groups.

enum.primary: Located below `<mdui-top-app-bar>`, used for switching between main application pages.
enum.secondary: Located within the page, used for switching between related content groups.

value.description: Specifies the active `<mdui-tab>` value.

placement.description:
Defines the tab position. Default is `top-start`. Possible values:

* `top-start`: Top left.
* `top`: Top centered.
* `top-end`: Top right.
* `bottom-start`: Bottom left.
* `bottom`: Bottom, centered.
* `bottom-end`: Bottom right.
* `left-start`: Left top.
* `left`: Left, centered.
* `left-end`: Left bottom.
* `right-start`: Right top.
* `right`: Right, centered.
* `right-end`: Right bottom.

fullWidth.description: If set, the tabs will fill the width of their parent element.

events.change: Emitted when the selected value changes.

variant.description:
Defines the text field variant. Default is `filled`. Possible values:

* `filled`: Text field with a background color for stronger visual emphasis.
* `outlined`: Text field with a border for subtler visual emphasis.

enum.filled: Text field with a background color for stronger visual emphasis.
enum.outlined: Text field with a border for subtler visual emphasis.

type.description:
Specifies the text field type. Default is `text`. Possible values:

* `text`: Standard text field.
* `number`: Allows numeric input only. Virtual keyboards on mobile devices show a numeric layout.
* `password`: Hides the password as you type.
* `url`: Validates URL format. Virtual keyboards on mobile devices show a URL-specific layout.
* `email`: Validates email format. Virtual keyboards on mobile devices show an email-specific layout.
* `search`: Shows a search icon on the Enter key in virtual keyboards.
* `tel`: Displays a phone keypad on virtual keyboards.
* `hidden`: Hides the control, but its value is still submitted to the server.
* `date`: Opens a date picker or a numeric scroll wheel for year, month, and day in supported browsers.
* `datetime-local`: Activates a date and time picker in supported browsers, without a time zone.
* `month`: Allows entering a year and month without a time zone.
* `time`: Allows time input without a time zone.
* `week`: Allows entering a year and week without a time zone.

enum.number: Allows only numeric input. Devices with virtual keyboards will display a numeric keyboard.
enum.password: Hides the password as you type.
enum.url: Validates URL format. Devices with virtual keyboards will display a URL-specific keyboard.
enum.email: Validates email format. Devices with virtual keyboards will display an email-specific keyboard.
enum.search: Shows a search icon on the Enter key in virtual keyboards.
enum.tel: Displays a phone keypad on virtual keyboards.
enum.hidden: Hides the control, but its value is still submitted to the server.
enum.date: Opens a date picker or a numeric scroll wheel for year, month, and day in supported browsers.
enum.datetime-local: Activates a date and time picker in supported browsers, excluding time zone.
enum.month: Allows entering a year and month, without a time zone.
enum.time: Allows time input, excluding time zone.
enum.week: Allows entering a year and week, without a time zone.

name.description: The name of the text field, which is submitted with form data.

value.description: The value of the text field, which is submitted with form data.

defaultValue.description: The default value. The text field resets to this value on form reset. JavaScript only.

helper.description: The helper text displayed at the bottom of the text field. Alternatively, use `slot="helper"`.

helperOnFocus.description: Shows the helper text only when the text field is focused.

clearIcon.description: Material Icons name shown on the right when the text field is clearable. Alternatively, use `slot="clear-icon"`.

endAligned.description: Aligns the text to the right.

prefix.description: The prefix text for the text field. It is only displayed when the text field is focused or has a value. Alternatively, use `slot="prefix"`.

suffix.description: The suffix text for the text field. It is only displayed when the text field is focused or has a value. Alternatively, use `slot="suffix"`.

icon.description: Material Icons name for the prefix icon of the text field. Alternatively, use `slot="icon"`.

endIcon.description: Material Icons name for the suffix icon of the text field. Alternatively, use `slot="end-icon"`.

errorIcon.description: Material Icons name displayed on the right side of the text field when form field validation fails. Alternatively, use `slot="error-icon"`.

form.description:
Associates the text field with a `<form>` element. Set this to the `id` of a `<form>` in the same document. If omitted, the text field uses its parent `<form>`, if any.

This lets the text field work with any form in the document, not just the one it is nested in.

required.description: The field must be filled out before the form is submitted.

rows.description: The number of rows in the text field.

autosize.description: Automatically adjusts the height of the text field based on its content.

minRows.description: The minimum number of rows when `autosize` is enabled.

maxRows.description: The maximum number of rows when `autosize` is enabled.

minlength.description: The minimum number of characters for input.

maxlength.description: The maximum number of characters for input.

counter.description: Displays the character count when `maxlength` is specified.

step.description: The step interval for increment/decrement when `type` is `number`.

pattern.description: The regular expression for form validation.

togglePassword.description: Adds a toggle button to show or hide the password when `type` is `password`.

showPasswordIcon.description: Material Icons name for the visible password toggle button. Alternatively, use `slot="show-password-icon"`.

hidePasswordIcon.description: Material Icons name for the hidden password toggle button. Alternatively, use `slot="hide-password-icon"`.

autocapitalize.description:
A non-standard iOS attribute for automatic capitalization. Possible values:

* `none`: Disables automatic capitalization.
* `sentences`: Capitalizes the first letter of each sentence.
* `words`: Capitalizes the first letter of each word.
* `characters`: Capitalizes all letters.

enum.sentences: Capitalizes the first letter of each sentence.
enum.words: Capitalizes the first letter of each word.

enterkeyhint.description:
Customizes the Enter key text or icon on the virtual keyboard. The effect varies by device and language. Possible values:

* `enter`: Inserts a new line, typically used in a multi-line text field.
* `done`: Indicates completion and closes the virtual keyboard.
* `go`: Navigates to the target of the entered text.
* `next`: Moves to the next text field.
* `previous`: Moves to the previous text field.
* `search`: Navigates to search results.
* `send`: Sends a text message.

enum.enter: Inserts a new line, typically used in a multi-line text field.
enum.done: Indicates input completion, closes the virtual keyboard.
enum.go: Navigates to the target of the entered text.
enum.next: Moves to the next text field.
enum.previous: Moves to the previous text field.

inputmode.description:
Customizes the virtual keyboard. Possible values:

* `none`: No virtual keyboard. Useful for custom input controls.
* `text`: Standard text input keyboard.
* `decimal`: Decimal input keyboard. This includes a period `.` or comma `,` and numbers.
* `numeric`: Numeric keyboard. This displays numbers 0–9.
* `tel`: Phone number keyboard. This includes numbers 0–9, asterisk `*`, and hash `#` keys.
* `search`: Search-optimized virtual keyboard. 'Search' appears on the submit button.
* `email`: Email-optimized virtual keyboard. This typically includes `@` and `.`.
* `url`: URL-optimized virtual keyboard. This typically includes `.`, `/`, and `#`.

enum.none: No virtual keyboard. This is useful for custom input controls.
enum.decimal: Decimal input keyboard. This includes a period `.` or comma `,` and numbers.
enum.tel: Phone number keyboard. This includes numbers 0–9, asterisk `*`, and hash `#` keys.
enum.search: Search-optimized virtual keyboard. 'Search' appears on the submit button.
enum.email: Email-optimized virtual keyboard. This typically includes `@` and `.`.
enum.url: URL-optimized virtual keyboard. This typically includes `.`, `/`, and `#`.

validity.description: A [`ValidityState`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ValidityState) object that represents the element's validity states.

validationMessage.description: Validation message. Empty string when valid.

valueAsNumber.description: Gets or sets the value as a `number`. Returns `NaN` if the value cannot be converted.

methods.select: Selects the content of the text field.
methods.setSelectionRange: Selects a specific range in the text field.
methods.setRangeText: Replaces a specific range in the text field with new text.
methods.checkValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event and returns `false`. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.reportValidity: Checks the validity of the form field. If it is invalid, it triggers an `invalid` event, returns `false`, and displays a validation message. If valid, it returns `true`.
methods.setCustomValidity: Sets a custom error message. If the message is non-empty, the field is considered invalid.

events.focus: Emitted when the text field gains focus.
events.blur: Emitted when the text field loses focus.
events.change: Emitted when the value changes and the text field loses focus.
events.invalid: Emitted when the form control's validity is checked and it does not meet the constraints.
events.clear: Emitted when the clear button is clicked. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.

slots.error-icon: Icon on the right side for validation failure.
slots.show-password-icon: Icon in the password visibility toggle button (show password state).
slots.hide-password-icon: Icon in the password visibility toggle button (hide password state).
slots.helper: Helper text shown below the field.

cssParts.error-icon: Icon on the right side for validation failure.
cssParts.label: Label text above the field.
cssParts.show-password-icon: Icon in the password visibility toggle button (show password state).
cssParts.hide-password-icon: Icon in the password visibility toggle button (hide password state).
cssParts.supporting: Container for supporting text at the bottom of the field, including helper, error, and counter.
cssParts.helper: Helper text shown below the field.
cssParts.error: Text at the bottom for error.
cssParts.counter: Character count in the bottom right corner.

variant.description:
Defines the tooltip variant. Default is `plain`. Possible values:

* `plain`: For simple, single-line text.
* `rich`: For tooltips with a title, body text, and action buttons.

enum.rich: For tooltips with a title, body text, and action buttons.

placement.description:
Sets the tooltip position. Default is `auto`. Possible values:

* `auto`: Automatically determined. For `variant="plain"`, prefers `top`; for `variant="rich"`, prefers `bottom-right`.
* `top-left`: Top left.
* `top-start`: Top start.
* `top`: Top center.
* `top-end`: Top end.
* `top-right`: Top right.
* `bottom-left`: Bottom left.
* `bottom-start`: Bottom start.
* `bottom`: Bottom center.
* `bottom-end`: Bottom end.
* `bottom-right`: Bottom right.
* `left-start`: Left start.
* `left`: Left center.
* `left-end`: Left end.
* `right-start`: Right start.
* `right`: Right center.
* `right-end`: Right end.

openDelay.description: The delay (in milliseconds) before the tooltip appears on hover.

closeDelay.description: The delay (in milliseconds) before the tooltip disappears on hover.

headline.description: Sets the tooltip title. Only applies when `variant="rich"`. Alternatively, use `slot="headline"`.

content.description: Sets the tooltip content. Alternatively, use `slot="content"`.

trigger.description:
Defines how the tooltip opens. Multiple space-separated values are supported. Possible values:

* `click`: Triggers on click.
* `hover`: Triggers on mouse hover.
* `focus`: Triggers on focus.
* `manual`: Can only be opened and closed programmatically; no other trigger methods can be specified.

enum.manual: Can only be opened and closed programmatically; no other trigger methods can be specified.

events.open: Emitted when the tooltip starts to open. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.opened: Emitted after the tooltip has opened and the animations are completed.
events.close: Emitted when the tooltip starts to close. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.closed: Emitted after the tooltip has closed and the animations are completed.

slots: Target element that triggers the tooltip. Only the first element in the `default` slot is used as the target.
slots.headline: Tooltip title. Only applies when `variant="rich"`.
slots.content: Tooltip content, which can include HTML. If you only need plain text, you can also use the `content` attribute.
slots.action: Action button at the bottom of the tooltip. Only available when `variant="rich"`.

cssProperties.--shape-corner-plain: Corner radius of the component when `variant="plain"`. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--shape-corner-rich: Corner radius of the component when `variant="rich"`. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--z-index: The CSS `z-index` value of the component.

mdui-top-app-bar.summary:
Top App Bar Component. ```html .. ..Title ..
.. ```

variant.description:
Defines the top app bar variant. Default is `small`. Possible values:

* `center-aligned`: A small app bar with a centered title.
* `small`: Small app bar.
* `medium`: Medium-sized app bar.
* `large`: Large-sized app bar.

enum.center-aligned: Small app bar with a centered title.

hide.description: Whether the top app bar is hidden.

shrink.description: Shrinks the app bar to the `small` variant. Only applies to `medium` or `large` variants.

scrollBehavior.description:
Defines the scroll behavior. Multiple space-separated values are accepted. Possible values:

* `hide`: Hides when scrolling.
* `shrink`: Shrinks when scrolling (for medium to large app bars).
* `elevate`: Increases elevation when scrolling.

events.show: Emitted when the top app bar starts to show. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.shown: Emitted after the top app bar has shown and animations are complete.
events.hide: Emitted when the top app bar starts to hide. Can be prevented with `event.preventDefault()`.
events.hidden: Emitted after the top app bar has hidden and animations are complete.

slots: Elements contained within the top app bar.

cssProperties.--shape-corner: The corner radius of the component. You can use a specific pixel value, but it is recommended to reference [design tokens](/docs/2/styles/design-tokens#shape-corner).
cssProperties.--z-index: The CSS `z-index` value of the component.

mdui-top-app-bar-title.summary:
Top App Bar Title Component. Use with the `` component. ```html .. ..Title ..
.. ```

slots: The title text of the top app bar.
slots.label-large: The title text when the top app bar is in the expanded state.

cssParts.label: The title text.
cssParts.label-large: The title text when the top app bar is in the expanded state.


mdui-prose.description: Apply this class to enhance typography styles for articles.

mdui-table.description: Apply this class to the `<table>` element to enhance its display style. It can also be applied to the parent element of the `<table>` to both enhance the table's styling and enable horizontal scrolling for it.
